sanskrit: अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः । अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च ॥
hindiTranslation: हे नींदको जीतनेवाले अर्जुन  सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके आदि, मध्य तथा अन्तमें भी मैं ही हूँ और प्राणियोंके अन्तःकरणमें आत्मरूपसे भी मैं ही स्थित हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: I am the Self, O Gudakesha, seated in the heart of all beings. I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings.
essence: Everything that exists is held within one enduring presence.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he lives within every being as the inner self and is present at every stage of existence: beginning, middle, and end.
simpleInsight: You are held inside something far larger than your private story.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna asks to hear more of Krishna’s divine manifestations. Krishna answers that he is not distant: he dwells in the heart of every being and is present at every stage of life, from origin to completion.
modernRelevance: You sit beside someone in a hospital corridor, watching a life change in silence. This verse says the deepest presence is already there, inside every beginning, middle, and ending.
translationLiteral: अहम् (I am) / आत्मा (the Self) / गुडाकेश (O Gudakesha) / सर्वभूताशयस्थितः (standing in the heart of all beings) / अहम् (I am) / आदिः (the beginning) / च (and) / मध्यम् (the middle) / च (and) / भूतानाम् (of beings) / अन्तः (the end) / एव (indeed) / च (and)
insight: It shifts attention from scattered appearances to the single presence running through all change.

quoteVariants.text: I am in every heart. I am the start, middle, and end of all beings.
All beings begin, endure, end in Me.
What lives in every heart is Me.
I am beginning, middle, and end.
